exemplar.title:
Investigating the Relationship Between Spring Elasticity and 
Reduced Force During a Collision

detailedExemplarData.title:
Investigating the Relationship Between Spring Elasticity and 
Reduced Force During a Collision

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clear and precise, with consistent units, significant figures, bestâfit equations and RÂ² values
Data processing is carried out accurately with correct sample calculations and no major arithmetic errors
weaknesses: Consideration of uncertainties has significant omissions (no propagation for impulse/force, missing systematic uncertainty discussion)
Minor formatting issues (figure numbering duplication, rounding inconsistencies) slightly reduce overall precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data recording and processing and the quality of data processing both clearly fall in the top band (5â6), but the treatment of uncertainties shows only partial consideration with significant omissions (band 3â4). Balancing these strengths and weaknesses results in a Data Analysis score of 4.

strengths: The research question is grounded in a precise, realâworld context using current vehicle safety statistics
Methodology is described in sufficient detail (ramp angle, release height, camera fps, repeats) to allow reproducibility
Methodological considerations are explained, linking spring selection and highâspeed video to error minimization
weaknesses: Exact camera model and mass calibration method are not specified, slightly limiting full reproducibility
A paragraph ends abruptly midâsentence and could be clarified to complete the contrast between stiffness levels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Research Design comfortably meet the 5â6 band. The context is highly specific and sustained, the method can be reproduced with minimal ambiguity, and methodological considerations are explained rather than merely listed. Minor omissions (camera model, calibration details) prevent a perfect topâband mark, so a score of 5 is awarded.

strengths: Conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis, quantitatively citing the inverse relationship and high RÂ² value
Connections to realâworld crumpleâzones and the theoretical framework are clear and relevant
weaknesses: Comparison to the broader scientific context remains qualitative; lacks quantitative benchmarking against published crashâtest data
Minor repetition in wording; could include numerical gradient comparison for greater depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion effectively justifies the findings and aligns with the analysis (band 5â6 for relevance and support) but the scientific context comparison is only at the âsome relevant comparisonâ level (band 3â4). Overall, the work sits in the upper part of the 3â4 band, yielding a score of 4.

strengths: Evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses (frameârate limits, friction, zero error) and describes their likely impacts
Proposed improvements are realistic and relevant (higherâfps camera, lowâfriction surfaces, automated sensors)
weaknesses: The relative impact of each weakness is not fully explained, leaving the reader uncertain which errors dominate
Improvement rationales are brief and would benefit from deeper explanation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands of Evaluation demonstrate specific descriptions of weaknesses and realistic improvements, placing the work in the 3â4 band. Explanations lack depth on relative impacts and detailed rationales, so a score of 4 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student places the research question squarely within the very specific context of modern vehicle safety â namely, the role of crumple-zones in reducing forces during head-on collisions. Current accident statistics, personal motivation and an explicit analogy between real crumple-zones and toy-car springs show a clear, relevant and non-generic grounding. Because the context is precise, relates directly to the variables under study and is consistently referred to throughout the introduction and hypothesis, the work aligns with the top-band descriptor. Minor stylistic issues do not detract from the specificity of the context, so a score at the lower end of the 5-6 band (5) is appropriate.

Uncertainties are calculated for collision-time using half-range and shown as error bars on one graph. However, uncertainties for velocity, mass, spring constant and propagated uncertainties for impulse/force are absent. No discussion of systematic uncertainty is attempted in the analysis section. This constitutes âevidence of consideration with significant omissionsâ, fitting the 3-4 band; the lower value 3 is selected.

Methodological considerations are explained rather than merely listed. The student justifies purchasing springs of identical length/diameter to isolate wire-diameter effects, discusses why five k-values and five repeats were selected, and links high-fps video to minimising timing error. Limitations such as friction, frame rate and manufacturing tolerances are recognised later, showing awareness of data sufficiency and relevance to the research question. Although propagation of uncertainty is not planned at this stage, the considerations are more than descriptive, meeting the âexplainedâ criterion; hence score 5 in the top band.

Specific weaknesses (frame rate limits, friction, zero error, environmental factors, limited range of k) are identified and their likely impacts are described in tables, but explanations of relative magnitude (which errors dominate) are brief. This fits âdescribes specific methodological weaknessesâ, upper band 3-4; score 4.

The step-by-step method, equipment list and annotated diagram provide enough quantitative detail (ramp angle 30Â°, release height 35 cm, 10 cm car separation, five repeats, 240 fps camera, identical 33 g cars) for another student to reproduce the setup with minimal interpretation. Controlled-variable explanations (mass, ramp surface, initial velocity, environment) further reduce ambiguity. While the exact model of camera and the frame calibration used in Tracker are not stated, these omissions are minor and do not prevent replication, justifying placement in the 5-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is awarded.

All data processing steps (average, half-range, Hooke-law spring constant, energy relation for compression, impulse, force) are shown with sample calculations that give correct numerical answers and units. Internal consistency between tables and graphs is good, and no major arithmetic errors are evident. As the processing appropriately supports the research question, the strand falls within the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects a small loss for minor rounding inconsistencies.

For each weakness the student proposes realistic and relevant improvements (e.g., higher-fps camera, automated sensors, wider spring range, alternative low-friction surfaces) with brief rationale, satisfying âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ. Consequently, the strand sits in the upper 3-4 range; score 4.

Raw-data tables contain units, significant figures are mostly consistent, processedâdata tables show derived quantities, and graphs include axis labels, units, best-fit equations and RÂ² values. Minor formatting issues (figure numbering duplication) slightly reduce precision, but both clarity and precision are largely met. Therefore a score of 5 inside the 5-6 band is justified.

The student connects findings to real-world crumple-zones and declining fatality statistics, showing ârelevant comparisonâ. However, the comparison remains qualitative; there is no quantitative benchmarking against published crash-test data or accepted k-value ranges for vehicle structures. The work therefore reaches the upper part of the 3-4 band; score 4.

The conclusion restates the observed inverse relationship and quantitatively cites the high RÂ² value, explicitly linking back to the hypothesis and derived theory. It uses processed results (trend lines, impulse and force calculations) as evidence, so the justification is clearly grounded in the analysis. Minor repetition and lack of numerical gradient comparison prevent full sophistication, but the work is still âfully consistent with analysisâ; score 5.

annotations.comment: Report the uncertainty in velocity measurement from Tracker to support later force calculations.
Specify the camera model and fps in the materials list to ensure full reproducibility of measurements.
Figure 1âs diagram is wellâannotated, showing ramp angle and release mechanism clearly.
Add component labels (e.g., spring, release latch) in figure caption to enhance clarity of setup.
Preliminary trials section effectively explains rationale for upgrading to 240 fps camera.
Processed data table presents $k$ values with consistent significant figures and units.
The title succinctly communicates the main variables and context of the investigation, guiding reader expectations effectively.
Excellent integration of U.S. accident statistics and fatality trends to situate the research question in a precise, realâworld context.
Clear statement of focus on spring constant, collision time, impulse and force with planned use of highâspeed video analysis.
The paragraph ends abruptly midâsentence; clarify and complete the contrast between high and low stiffness springs.
Good explanation of how varying spring compression models a crumple zone by extending collision duration.
Consider specifying the exact mass of each toy car and calibration method for the scale to improve reproducibility.
Effective description of impulseâmomentum theorem and its relevance to crumpleâzone function.
Impulse equation $J=F\Delta t$ is clearly highlighted, reinforcing analytical framework.
Hookeâs law equation $F=-kx$ is accurately presented, linking spring mechanics to force analysis.
Use of shearâmodulus formula for spring constant shows depth in background research.
Research question is precise, linking spring elasticity explicitly to impulse and force in a toyâcar collision.
Background could be strengthened by citing typical impact durations in real vehicle crash tests.
Newtonâs second law equation is accurately formatted and numbered, demonstrating clear theoretical grounding.
Derivation of $x_{max}=\sqrt{\frac{mv^2}{k}}$ is correct and clearly justified by energy conservation.
Algebraic manipulation to express $F_{avg}$ could be refined to directly show dependence on $1/\sqrt{k}$.
Graphical hypothesis statement clearly articulates expected inverse relationship with $1/\sqrt{k}$.
Dependent variable is specified with clear measurement protocol using video analysis.
Risk assessment table is comprehensive and wellâstructured, covering safety, ethical, and environmental aspects.
Sample calculation for spring constant is detailed and mathematically correct.
Include propagation of uncertainty for impulse based on uncertainties in $t_{avg}$ and $x_{max}$.
Force calculations lack uncertainty; specify $\,\Delta F$ to reflect measurement precision.
Uncertainty section identifies ranges and distinguishes random vs systematic sources.
Random error table offers realistic improvements such as higherâfps cameras and multiple spring averages.
Systematic error table is thorough; calibration and sensor improvements are wellâmatched to each error type.
Limitation discussion acknowledges toyâcar model shortcoming; quantify how this simplification affects result magnitude.
Average collision time formula and sample mean are correctly calculated with appropriate significant figures.
Uncertainty in $t_{avg}$ is correctly determined using halfârange but consider propagating through later calculations.
Raw data table includes all trials and uncertainties; well organized and comprehensive.
Graph of time vs diameter illustrates trend well; consider adding error bars from $t_{avg}\pm\Delta t$.
High $R^2$ interpretation shows strong model fit; good awareness of statistical significance.
Sample $x_{max}$ calculation is accurate; demonstrates correct use of $x_{max}=\sqrt{mv^2/k}$.
Conclusion restates the inverse relationship, links back to hypothesis, and cites high $R^2$ value effectively.
Comparison to modern vehicle crash data is qualitative; include quantitative benchmarking for stronger context.
Strengths table effectively highlights controlled variables, standardized procedure, and realâworld relevance.
Extension proposal regarding temperature impact is interesting; consider preliminary calibration of spring modulus at different temperatures.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
